seed: trust vs suspicion; signal_vs_noise; split_vertical; diagonal_fall; stairs against ash_field; pressure_border; soft_glow_vs_hard_shadow; line; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 492; target batch count 500
title: Symmetry in Conflict
reasoning: This abstract artwork captures the emotional tension between trust and suspicion in a spiral layout. The chosen palette of deep blacks and purples conveys a sense of looming uncertainty, while the soft gold and pale green add glimmers of hope and assurance. The primary collision occurs in the center, where the dense dark colors meet the lighter hues, suggesting a struggle between these two emotional states. The diagonal of the color placement creates a pull, reminiscent of stairs, yet soft edges represent resilience amidst that tension. The broken pixels are strategically placed to evoke a scarred feeling, reinforcing the theme of imperfection in trust. The overall design suggests a movement towards the light, reflecting a journey from suspicion into trust, allowing the viewer to engage with the underlying narrative of conflict and resolution.

layout.name: spiral_pressure
layout.description: Marks coil toward or away from a small off-center point.

composition.name: symmetry_vs_break
composition.idea: A nearly mirrored image has one wrong or corrupted side.

referenceExample.title: Lonely Signal
referenceExample.pattern: single bright dot with faint surrounding echo

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
